sources.title: 80 PLUS program load-point requirements
sources.licenseOrUseNote: Public 80 PLUS program requirements. Referenced target efficiency values only.
sources.evidenceSummary: 80 PLUS program requires minimum efficiency at 20/50/100% load for 115 V and 230 V input. Generic curves use these as conservative planning targets.

sources.title: AIO liquid cooler pump/fan power methodology
sources.licenseOrUseNote: Maintainer methodology. CC BY 4.0.
sources.evidenceSummary: Generic AIO cooler pump draws ~3-5 W sustained with peaks to 8 W during startup. Fan power scales with radiator size; 240 mm ~3 W, 360 mm ~5 W.

sources.title: ATX12V Power Supply Design Guide (Intel reference)
sources.licenseOrUseNote: Reference document; cited for ATX slot and platform power conventions.
sources.evidenceSummary: ATX specification defines typical platform board power consumption of 30-60 W depending on slot population. Used to derive the generic-atx-platform-baseline values.

sources.title: Crucial MX500 product page
sources.licenseOrUseNote: Manufacturer-published specification. Referenced fact values only.
sources.evidenceSummary: Crucial MX500 1 TB 2.5-inch SATA SSD draws up to 3.0 W active read with sustained ~2.5 W.

sources.title: Crucial T700 product page
sources.licenseOrUseNote: Manufacturer-published specification. Referenced fact values only.
sources.evidenceSummary: Crucial T700 2 TB PCIe 5.0 NVMe SSD consumes up to 12 W active read with sustained ~8 W.

sources.title: DDR5 SDRAM Standard (JEDEC JESD79-5)
sources.licenseOrUseNote: Industry standard; cited for module-level power conventions.
sources.evidenceSummary: DDR5 module power is typically 3-5 W per UDIMM at sustained throughput. RDIMMs add ~1-2 W for register power.

sources.title: 3.5-inch HDD typical power consumption (planning values)
sources.licenseOrUseNote: Maintainer methodology. CC BY 4.0.
sources.evidenceSummary: Typical 3.5-inch 7200 RPM consumer HDDs consume 7-10 W sustained at load with ~2 W idle. Used for generic-3-5-hdd-baseline.

sources.title: NVMe Form Factor Specification (PCIe power budget)

sources.title: PSU power planning baseline methodology
sources.licenseOrUseNote: Maintainer methodology document. CC BY 4.0.
sources.evidenceSummary: Generic platform/memory/storage/cooling/network baselines are aggregated planning values that combine chipset TDP, slot count, and typical accessory power. Methodology details live in data/METHODOLOGY.md.

sources.title: Samsung 990 PRO product page
sources.licenseOrUseNote: Manufacturer-published specification. Referenced fact values only.
sources.evidenceSummary: Samsung 990 PRO 2 TB has a max power consumption of 8.1 W active read, lower idle. Sustained ~6 W.

sources.title: Seagate Exos X20 product page
sources.licenseOrUseNote: Manufacturer-published specification. Referenced fact values only.
sources.evidenceSummary: Seagate Exos X20 20 TB enterprise HDD draws ~9.4 W read/write sustained, ~6 W idle.

sources.title: WD_BLACK SN850X product page
sources.licenseOrUseNote: Manufacturer-published specification. Referenced fact values only.
sources.evidenceSummary: WD_BLACK SN850X 2 TB draws up to 7.4 W peak under load with sustained ~5.5 W.

sources.title: Wi-Fi NIC power planning methodology
sources.licenseOrUseNote: Maintainer methodology. CC BY 4.0.
sources.evidenceSummary: Generic Wi-Fi 6/6E M.2 cards consume 1-3 W idle and 4-6 W during active transmission. Methodology in data/METHODOLOGY.md.
